data.body:
# Validation: does the Gerrit code-review gate achieve accumulated attestation for stacked stories?

Scenario validated (user ask): epic with stories A, B, C. Agent RED reviews+lands approval for A, agent BLUE for B (separate worktrees, both based on main), agent GREEN builds C on top of combined A+B and wants (1) a review of only C's delta, (2) a combined landing carrying attestations for all three, (3) no large re-review of A+B+C combined at merge time.

## Verdict: PARTIALLY ACHIEVED — the per-change delta review works; attestation accumulation breaks at the "combine A+B" step, by deliberate design.

Evidence base: epics dowdy-swear-bird (b744), alto-fruit-punch (d251), smoky-flare-slug (1fa8); session logs slap-tripe-vat, watery-fray-waltz, vivid-mull-need; stories bluff-crane-pixel (S3), cogent-strut-kid (S4b), low-cloud-wreck (WS1), virid-trench-stone (WS6), van-withe-folly (WS6-b), weedy-lath-abbey (WS7); ADR 0013; infra/gerrit/project.config, infra/compose/gerrit.config, infra/gerrit/replication.config, infra/gerrit/webhooks.config; src/rebar/review_bot/ (voter.py, gerrit_client.py, adapter.py, reconcile.py, app.py); src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py, src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py.

## What WORKS
1. Review-of-delta-only: the bot reviews each change's own parent-relative diff (GET /a/changes/{id}/revisions/{rev}/patch = format-patch vs parent; gerrit_client.py). For stacked change C with parents A<-B, the reviewed diff is C's delta only, while clone_change_ref materializes the full tree (incl. unmerged A+B) for detector/file-tool context. GREEN's goal (1) is fully met.
2. Per-change attestation + atomic landing: submit requirement `label:LLM-Review=MAX AND -has:unresolved` evaluates per change; submitWholeTopic=true lands a multi-change feature atomically (proven live: changes 22/23, S3 AC4). Chain submit from the top also works (stock Gerrit, per-change gates all enforced). Goal (2) is met as a chain/topic of three individually-attested commits — NOT as one squashed change (a squash is a new change with no votes => full combined review, i.e. the exact thing to avoid).
3. No combined mega-review ever runs: the bot has zero chain/topic awareness (voter._extract reads only change+patchset ids); every review is per-change delta-scoped. Goal (3)'s literal fear ("large re-review of A+B+C combined") cannot happen.

## What BREAKS
4. The combine step wipes votes. A and B were built as siblings on main; combining them requires rebasing B onto A (new patchset for B). copyCondition = changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE only — TRIVIAL_REBASE was DELIBERATELY excluded (S3 "peer-pattern correction"; ADR 0013: "a rebase always re-triggers review"; red-team flag: copying across a moved base "would certify a tree the LLM never reviewed"). So B's +1 is dropped at assembly; the webhook auto-triggers a fresh FULL four-pass review of B's (delta-scoped) new patchset. Cost: N-1 re-reviews to assemble N sibling stories; plus nondeterministic risk the re-review casts -1 with new findings that GREEN (not BLUE) must remediate.
5. Cascade on any base motion: if main advances (or A is amended to address a finding), the whole stack above must rebase => all descendant votes wipe => per-change re-reviews down the chain. Standard Gerrit also refuses submitting a child based on an outdated ancestor patchset, forcing exactly this rebase.
6. Semantic tension in the goal itself: B's +1 certified B-on-main, not B-on-A. Accumulation across a base move is unsound as specified — the current design chose soundness (re-review) over accumulation. Any fix must address the interaction review, not just carry the vote.
7. No accumulation record in rebar: no "code-review" attestation kind exists (only plan-review / completion-verifier); the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar is only emitted when target_ticket is supplied and the Gerrit voter supplies none. The volatile Gerrit label is the sole artifact.
8. Residual unknowns: project submit type is unset in-repo (inherits All-Projects, presumably MERGE_IF_NECESSARY) => history shape of a chain submit on GitHub main is unpinned; outdated-ancestor + topic-union submit mechanics undocumented. The open epic smoky-flare-slug AC4 already records "carry vs re-run across trivial rebases" as an undesigned question for the future Verified vote.

## Design-intent finding
The multi-agent stacked-worktree scenario was never designed: "stacked diffs" appears once in slap-tripe-vat's research-basis list; rebar search "stacked"/"relation chain" has zero Gerrit design hits. The shipped mechanism for multi-story features is topics (atomic landing), and the shipped no-re-pay lever is copyCondition NO_CODE_CHANGE (message-only amends). Everything else re-reviews.

## Options to close the gap (not actioned)
A. Workflow discipline (no code): build stories as a chain from the start (BLUE stacks on A, GREEN on B) under one rebar-<feature> topic; votes survive until main moves. Tradeoff: serializes agents, defeats independent worktrees; still cascades on amend-of-ancestor.
B. Add changekind:TRIVIAL_REBASE to copyCondition: cheap, restores accumulation for byte-identical rebases. Tradeoff: reopens the red-team hole (certifies a tree never reviewed against its new base) — rejected once already.
C. Interaction-scoped re-review on TRIVIAL_REBASE: carry nothing, but replace the full four-pass re-review with a narrowed "does this delta still hold on the new base" pass — mirrors the plan-review epic boil-golem-veto's narrowed delta-re-review story (grace-thud-feint). Middle ground: sound + cheap; new capability to build.
D. Pin the submit strategy + document the chain/topic convention for epics in CONTRIBUTING.md (independent hygiene regardless of A-C).
